strengths: Demonstrated consistent ability to pick up and manipulate objects with both instruments
Maintained steady camera positioning throughout most transfer attempts
Showed good bilateral coordination during mid-air transfers
weaknesses: Failed to complete the full task cycle by returning all objects to their original positions
Lost track of task completion requirements during the transfer sequence
Did not establish a clear systematic approach to ensure all six objects were properly transferred

drills.name: Complete cycle rehearsal
drills.description: Practice the full task sequence slowly, counting objects aloud and verifying all six return to starting positions before considering the task complete.
drills.targets_weakness: Failed to complete the full task cycle.

drills.name: Object tracking checkpoint drill
drills.description: Transfer three objects to the opposite side, pause to count and verify positions, then complete the return transfers with verbal confirmation.
drills.targets_weakness: Lost track of task completion requirements.

drills.name: Systematic transfer pattern practice
drills.description: Establish a consistent left-to-right or right-to-left transfer order and practice maintaining this pattern for both outbound and return phases.
drills.targets_weakness: Did not establish a clear systematic approach.

progression_note: At the intermediate level, focus on developing reliable task completion habits and systematic approaches rather than speed. Your transfer mechanics are solid, but task awareness needs improvement to ensure consistent scoring.
